pi_items.source: Task description specifies export to /home/ga/Documents/airfoils/ directory with filename naca4415_bluntTE.dat

Task description explicitly states 'Trailing edge gap: 2.0% of chord', which equals 0.02 as a chord fraction. Verified against QBlade documentation and aerodynamic literature on trailing edge modifications.
Tolerance of ±0.008 (±0.8%) creates acceptable range [0.012, 0.028] or [1.2%, 2.8%], which is reasonable for airfoil geometry tolerance in manufacturing and design. Consistent with QBlade precision.
NACA 4415 airfoil nomenclature: 4=camber %, 4=camber position (40%), 15=thickness %. Verified against NACA 4-digit airfoil specification. Generated NACA 4415 coordinates confirm 15.56% thickness.
Tolerance of ±0.02 (±2%) creates acceptable range [0.13, 0.17] or [13%, 17%]. Generated NACA 4415 has 15.76% thickness, well within bounds. Standard tolerance for airfoil profile geometry.
Minimum 30 coordinate points is reasonable for valid airfoil profile. Standard airfoil files (NACA 4412: 35 points, generated NACA 4415: 199 points) all exceed this minimum.

privileged_info_summary: NACA 4415 is a standard 4-digit airfoil with 4% maximum camber at 40% chord position and 15% maximum thickness. The task requires generating this airfoil in QBlade and applying a 2% trailing edge gap with 80% chord blending distance, then exporting to /home/ga/Documents/airfoils/naca4415_bluntTE.dat. All metadata parameters are verified: target thickness 15% (within ±2% tolerance), target TE gap 2% (within ±0.8% tolerance), minimum 30 coordinate points. These specifications are consistent with NACA aeronautical standards, QBlade software capabilities, and wind turbine blade manufacturing practices.
